`area` and `perimeter` just do the math and return it: `return self.width * self.height` and `return 2 * (self.width + self.height)`.
`Square.__init__` only needs one line: `super().__init__(side, side)`. This calls `Rectangle.__init__` and sets both `width` and `height` to `side`.
For `Square.__str__`, use `f"Square({self.width})"` — `self.width` works because `super().__init__` already set it.
